finding.contract_claim: document.getElementById("root") returns HTMLElement | null, so the non-null assertion `document.getElementById("root")!` passed to ReactDOM.createRoot will raise at runtime if the element is missing.
finding.observed_behavior: The code asserts non-null with `!` instead of a runtime check; the accompanying comment states the element is known to exist in index.html (standard React 18 bootstrapping pattern).
finding.triage_reasoning:
Reality: The `!` non-null assertion is intentional and explicitly documented by the inline comment: 'we know the element exists in index.html.' This is universally accepted React 18 bootstrapping. If `#root` were absent the app would crash at startup under any environment, which is caught during development and CI rather than silently in production.

Significance: This is an ecosystem convention — virtually every CRA / Vite React 18 scaffold uses this exact pattern. Replacing it with a runtime null-check and error throw would be marginally safer but is not an improvement meaningful enough to action.

The claim does not meet the significance predicate; it is a false positive on a deliberate, commented, and universally accepted idiom.
